name: Clock Multiplier
description: This algorithm is a simple clock multiplier, generating a faster clock from a slower one. The output clock rate is updated on every clock received, except that the clock duration is limited to at most double each time. This is mainly to prevent the output clock rate changing when the input clock is paused and restarted, which would otherwise be interpreted as a really long clock pulse.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Low voltage
parameters.description: The output voltage when the clock is low/inactive.

parameters.name: High voltage
parameters.description: The output voltage when the clock is high/active.

parameters.name: ES-5 Expander
parameters.description: If set, the ES-5 expander header to use for output instead of the bus specified by the Clock output parameter. 1 means the ES-5 itself.

output_ports.name: ES-5 Output
output_ports.description: If using an ES-5 expander output, sets which of the 8 outputs on the expander to use.
